intro.title: Atomic Vectors
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Atomic Vectors is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.

challenges.title: Easy: Use c(
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `c(` related to **Atomic Vectors**.

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply Atomic Vectors
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **Atomic Vectors**.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `c(` and `typeof` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Atomic Vectors** in a short script using `c(`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Atomic Vectors** (include `c(`).

questions.prompt: What best describes Atomic Vectors?

choices.label: A core R concept for data analysis

questions.prompt: Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from Atomic Vectors.

intro.title: Inspecting Structure
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Inspecting Structure is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
df <- data.frame(x = 1:3, y = c("a", "b", "c"))
str(df)
summary(df)

challenges.title: Easy: Use str(
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `str(` related to **Inspecting Structure**.

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: Apply Inspecting Structure
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write code demonstrating **Inspecting Structure**.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `str(` and `summary` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Inspecting Structure** in a short script using `str(`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Inspecting Structure** (include `str(`).
